title: YEEES 24: Delft 2018
summary: Hosted by Delft University of Technology (Delft, The Netherlands).
description: On April 12th and 13th 2018, Delft University of Technology (Netherlands) hosted the spring edition of the Young Energy Economists & Engineers Seminar. Special thanks go to João Gorenstein Dedecca and his team for their personal effort in the organization. 22 PhD students for all over Europe took part in discussions concerning the papers of their peers.
PRESENTATIONS
• Frederik Banis (Technical University of Denmark) Utilizing flexibility in microgrids using model predictive control
• Selma Causevic (Delft University of Technology)
• Michael Chesser (Dublin Institute of Technology) Is empowering Irish energy citizens working?
• Isaac Gonzalez (Pontificia Universidad Comillas) Proactive bi-level GEPTEP with storage considerations
• Sam Hamels (Ghent University) Replacing fossil-fuelled peakers with batteries – a security of supply perspective
• Ann-Katrin Hanke (Karlsruhe Institute of Technology) Reducing cost uncertainty by refunding prequalification costs in auctions for renewable energy support
• Paul-Hervé Tamokoue Kamga (Mines ParisTech) Home energy efficiency investments in response to energy price variations: evidence from France
• Cyril Martin De Lagarde (Université Paris-Dauphine) Drivers and diffusion of residential photovoltaics in France
• Paul Malliet (OFCE-Sciences Po) Estimating the elasticity of substitution between capital and energy from abatement curves: the case of the French industry
• Carla Mendes (University of Basel) Pricing reliability – on optimal imbalance prices
• Jelle Meus (KU Leuven) An international market for tradable green certificates: the impact of transmission congestion on efficiency and distributional welfare effects
• Longjian Piao (Delft University of Technology) Electricity market for direct current distribution systems: a survey of the design space
• Anna Garcia-Teruel (The University of Edinburgh) Integrating socio-economic scenarios into power system modelling: a case study of the energy transition in Europe
• Charlotte Scouflaire (Université Paris Dauphine) Information disclosure choice: Condorcet winning public information applied to capacity markets in liberalized power systems
• Nina Voulis (Delft University of Technology) Rethinking European energy taxation to incentivise consumer demand response participation
• Meng Wang (Delft University of Technology) Efficient heating and cooling approaches by using the novel ionic liquids within NH3 absorption systems
• Jens Weibezahn (Technische Universität Berlin) Joulia.jl – a large-scale spatial open-source electricity sector model using the Julia language

title: YEEES 25: Florence 2018
venue: European University Institute - Florence School of Regulation
location: Via della Badia dei Roccettini 9, Fiesole, 50014, FI
summary: Hosted by the Florence School of Regulation (FSR) at the European University Institute (EUI) (Florence, Italy).
description: On November 8th and 9th 2018, the 25th Young Energy Economists and Engineers Seminar (YEEES) will be hosted by the Florence School of Regulation (FSR) at the European University Institute (EUI) (Florence, Italy). The seminar gives PhD students the opportunity to present their working papers and receive feedback from their peers and senior reviewers in a friendly and constructive atmosphere.

For each paper, two opponents will be selected before the seminar: one senior researcher and one of the participating PhD students. This means, every participant will present his or her work and also review another PhD student’s work. Senior reviewers will come both from industry and academia and the review assignments will be made according to the areas of expertise of both senior and PhD reviewers. For the presentation of each paper, we plan to offer 20 minutes. Then, each opponent will have around 5-10 minutes to present her critical review and to give constructive comments. This is followed by questions and remarks from the audience.
• Authors are requested to register and send their abstract by the 15th of September. Please limit the abstract to 2000 characters.
• On the 20th of September, authors will be notified of their acceptance (seats are limited).

• Full papers (consistent finished or draft version) have to be handed-in by the 15th of October. Please limit the paper to 20 pages (excluding appendix), single space, 10 point font. Papers should be sent to YEEESFlorence@gmail.com.
• Papers are sorted by the organizers and distributed to two peer reviewers. We do not publish papers.
• People wishing to attend, but not present a paper can so (if there are seats left) by registering until October 15th.
importantDates: September 15, 2018: abstract submission and registration authors

title: YEEES 26: Leuven 2019
summary: Hosted by KU Leuven (Leuven, Belgium).
description: On June 3 and 4, 2019, the 26th Young Energy Economists and Engineers Seminar (YEEES) will be hosted by KU Leuven (Leuven, Belgium). The seminar gives PhD students and early-career researchers the opportunity to present their working papers and receive feedback from peers and senior reviewers in a friendly, constructive, and interdisciplinary atmosphere.

For each paper, two opponents will be selected before the seminar: one senior researcher and one of the participating PhD students. This means that every participant will present his or her work and also review another PhD student’s work. Senior reviewers will come both from academia and industry and the review assignments will be made according to the areas of expertise of both senior and PhD reviewers. For the presentation of each paper 20 minutes will be available. Then, each opponent will have around 5-10 minutes to present her critical review and to give constructive comments. This is followed by questions and remarks from the audience.
• Authors are requested to register and send their abstract by April 11. Please limit the abstract to 2000 characters.
• On the April 15, authors will be notified of their acceptance (seats are limited).

• Full papers (consistent finished or draft version) have to be handed in by May 15. Please limit the paper to 20 pages (excluding appendix), single space, 10 point font. Papers should be sent to yeees.leuven2019@kuleuven.be.
• Papers are sorted by the organizers and distributed to two peer reviewers. We do not publish papers.
• People wishing to attend, but not present a paper can so (if there are seats left) by registering until May 15.
importantDates: April 11, 2019: abstract submission and registration of authors
program: The program is unfortnately not available online.

description: On October 29 and 30, 2026, the 38th Young Energy Economists and Engineers Seminar (YEEES) will be hosted by the Faculty of Technology, Policy and Management at Delft University of Technology (Delft, The Netherlands). The seminar gives Ph.D. students the opportunity to present their working papers and receive feedback from their peers and senior reviewers in a friendly and constructive atmosphere.

For each paper, two reviewers will be selected before the seminar: one senior researcher and one of the participating Ph.D. students. This means every participant will present his or her work and review another Ph.D. student’s work. Senior reviewers will come from both industry and academia, and the review assignments will be made according to the areas of expertise of both senior and Ph.D. reviewers. For the presentation of each paper, we plan to offer 20 minutes. Then, each reviewer will have around 5-10 minutes to present their critical review and give constructive comments. This is followed by questions and remarks from the audience.
• Authors are requested to register and send their abstracts by July 12. Please limit the abstract to 2000 characters.
• By July 17, authors will be notified of their acceptance (seats are limited).
• Full papers (consistent finished or draft version) must be handed in by October 11. Please limit the paper to 20 pages (excluding the appendix), single-spaced, 10-point font.
• Papers are sorted by the organizers and distributed to two peer reviewers. We do not publish papers. Junior reviewers have to submit their feedback by October 26.
• People wishing to attend without presenting a paper can do so (if there are seats left) by registering by October 26.
importantDates: July 12, 2026: abstract submission and registration of authors
October 26, 2026: feedback submission (as a junior reviewer of another participant’s paper)
program: You will find the program here after the notification of acceptance deadline.
